title: Whole numbers and half truths
author: Rukmini S
thoughts: I am grateful to have bought this book on a whim. I learnt about how faulty the systems for collecting data are in india, how easily misrepresentated data can be even with accurate data collection and how easily propoganda can convince you something is true when it is not. This book checked my privilege in a way nothing else did. I hope to contribute to data for india one day

title: Build
author: Tony Fadell
thoughts: Must read for designers or anybody that wants to make things.

title: Observe, collect, draw
author: Giorgia Lupi and Stefanie Posavec
thoughts: I found joy in visualising personal data through this book. It has helped me consume mindfully and articulate my feelings beautifully. Data viz can be so fun and fulfilling, highly recommend this book to everyone.

title: Thinking in Systems
author: Donella H. Meadows
thoughts: A gift to everyone that thinks.

title: Thinking with Type
author: Ellen Lupton
thoughts: I'm scared of typography and this book made me feel unscared of it.

title: On writing
author: Stephen King
thoughts: I picked up 'The Institute' by Stephen King in 2018 and have been in awe of him since, I immediately wanted to know his process and I was gifted an autobiography + making the practice of writing everyday in this book, highly recommend to anyone wanting to make their writing better!

title: Psalm for the wild built
author: Becky Chambers
thoughts: Hiking + meditation + solarpunk, if I could gift this to everyone I meet, I would.

title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
author: Gabrielle Zevin
thoughts: Made me fall in love with videogames all over again. I have gamed/ watched my brother game since my inception, I love video games and I'd stopped playing after moving out, this made me want to play games again and look at game mechanics. I hope to make my own game some day.

title: Radiosilence
author: Alice Oseman
thoughts: My favourite fiction piece I come back to for comfort. It's about online communities, art, fiction and most importantly friendship

title: Sharp objects
author: Gillian Flynn
thoughts: In a way this book is feminist more than any other, there is cruelty depicted in a way between mother and daughters that I have always felt but never seen articulated before. I love it. I want to read more horror that encaspulates the violent existence of being a woman

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
thoughts: I dipped back into classics with this and I am appalled by how articulate it is about feelings I didn't even know I had. I will be reading more classics this year all thanks to Anna Karenina

title: Swann's way
author: Marcel Proust
thoughts: In gilmore girls, Max Medina says that everyone should read Proust everyday hence I was inspired to pick it up. It's unique in its articulation of thought and I'm learning a lot about writing train of thought.

title: Almond
author: Won Pyung Song
thoughts: I need to read more translated works, and more books about friendship. It was wholesome and kind.
